rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF IRENE PAOLINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/12/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) An allegation regarding notice to the VA is missing from paragraph 3 of the petition. 2) Based on the petitioner's time sheets submitted with the past two fee petitions, the petitioner appears to render 2-3 hours of services on average each month. Is the petitioner's request for fees on account based on his anticipating more than 2-3 hours of services per month after 08/01/2019 ? 3) Has the petitioner filed a proposed hourly fee schedule or statement of compensation in accordance with Probate Code §§ 2643.1(a) and 2614.7? 4) Was notice of how to file an objection under § 2643.1(c)(1) given? 5) Were notices under § 2643.1(c)(2) given to the court investigator? 6) What is the current balance of the conservatee's funds set aside and held in the attorney-client trust account maintained by the petitioner's attorneys? *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BLANDINA D. PADLAN REVOCABLE TRUST OF 5/16/00 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay Orders Entered On March 11, 2019 And The Orders Entered On December 2019
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Stay Orders Entered on March 11, 2019 and the Orders Entered on December 20, 2019 is denied. Neither CCP 404.5 nor CRC Rule 3.515 are applicable to these proceedings. December 20, 2019 orders were not automatically stayed by the December 23, 2019 Notice of Appeal. The March 14, 2019 order was not listed on the December 23, 2019 Notice of Appeal. Under Probate Code 1310 (e), "an appeal shall not stay the operation and effect of [?] an order directing payment of money unless" either (1) an undertaking is posted under CCP 917.1. Under CCP 917.1(a): "Unless an undertaking is given, the perfecting of an appeal shall not stay enforcement of the judgment or order in the trial court if the judgment or order is for [?]: Money or the payment of money, whether consisting of a special fund or not, and whether payable by the appellant or another party to the action." Each of the December 2019 orders now on appeal are, in whole or in part, orders for money or payment of money, which puts them under the exception language of Probate Code 1310(e) and CCP 917.1(a). No undertaking has been posted in this matter. Due to the nature of the March 14, 2019 and December 20, 2019 orders, the court cannot exercise its discretion to stay the subject orders under CCP 918. Under CCP 918, "the trial court may stay the enforcement of any judgment or order," except, "[i]f the enforcement of the judgment or order would be stayed on appeal only by the giving of an undertaking, a trial court shall not have power, without the consent of the adverse party, to stay the enforcement thereof pursuant to this section for a period which extends for more than 10 days beyond the last date on which a notice of appeal could be filed." The last date on which a notice of appeal could be filed for the December 20, 2019 orders or the March 14, 2019 order has passed, as has the subsequent ten-day period the court could enter without either an undertaking or consent of the adverse party. Per Ms. Graves' declaration in reply to Petitioner's opposition to this motion, the trust funds in the blocked account have been distributed in accordance with the December 20, 2019 Order for Withdrawal of Funds from Blocked Account, which was not stayed or invalidated by the Notice of Appeal (see CCP 683.010). Based on these facts, the court will not order an undertaking at this juncture. The Amended Motion to Stay the order of December 20, 2019, Order Harald Westendorf to Return Monies to the Trust, Order the Balance of Funds Distributed Returned to Trust filed on June 30, and the relief requested in the June 4 and June 22 filings, are not properly before the court at this hearing and will not be addressed. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 14, 2020.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN MARIE ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1. The accounting is $1,662.48 out of balance. 2. Many preliminary distributions were made, including personal property and cash exceeding $1.6M, without prior court approval. Travel costs were paid without prior court order (total ~$4k). 3. Many individuals who have relationships with administrators were retained to provide services to the estate without prior court approval. 4. There are two annuities that Petitioners allege cannot be liquidated and requests court order distributing the monthly income of $149.60 to co-petitioner Judith Anderson-Mason and ordering her to distribute half of it to co-petitioner Cynthia Anderson. 5. Petitioners request apportionment of attorneys' fees. 6. Cash has not been verified.

rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required pursuant to CRC 7.452. See also LRSF 14.3(A)(3) requiring counsel's appearance even if a continuance is needed to correct procedural issues. The Court is to determine whether a new sale should be ordered due to the following issues: 1) When the petition was filed, the reported sale had not been made.
